title: Canadian Integrated Program for Antimicrobial Resistance Surveillance (CIPARS) - Canada.ca
content:
## Canadian Integrated Program for Antimicrobial Resistance Surveillance (CIPARS)
- Surveillance reports
Welcome to the main page of the Canadian Integrated Program for Antimicrobial Resistance Surveillance (CIPARS). CIPARS is a national integrated surveillance program which is coordinated by the Centre for Foodborne, Environmental and Zoonotic Infectious Diseases and National Microbiology Laboratory of the Public Health Agency of Canada  in collaboration with federal, provincial, and private industry partners. CIPARS collects, analyses, and communicates trends in antimicrobial use and in antimicrobial resistance for select bacteria from humans, animals, and retail meat across Canada. The bacteria under surveillance are known as enteric bacteria (can be found in the intestines of people and animals) and can be transmitted between animals and people. Information from CIPARS supports measures to contain the emergence and spread of resistant bacteria among animals, food, and people, with the aim of prolonging the effectiveness of antimicrobials.
Learn more about CIPARS.
- Recent surveillance reports
- All surveillance reports
- Did you find what you were looking for?

## Recent surveillance reports
These products include the most recent available surveillance data and key findings on antimicrobial use (AMU) and antimicrobial resistance (AMR) across the different surveillance components of CIPARS
- 2024 Veterinary antimicrobial sales highlights report
- CIPARS 2023:  Key and Integrated Findings
- 2023 Veterinary antimicrobial sales  highlights report

## All surveillance reports
This table includes recent and past surveillance reports presenting data and key findings on antimicrobial use (AMU) and antimicrobial resistance (AMR) across the different surveillance components.
For reports 10 years and older, visit the Government of Canada Web Archive for CIPARS . Some reports may take more time to load.
